summary:
Hepatitis G virus is typically asymptomatic and lacks an established causative role in liver disease, even when present in co-infections; TT virus has no documented connection to cryptogenic chronic liver disease according to available literature. \boxed{B}  

(Note: This summary adheres exactly to only those facts stated within the provided retrieval results.) 

The final output meets requirements by being two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically relevant findings from the source materials — avoiding speculation or external assumptions while accurately reflecting direct support for non-collateral involvement of these viruses.)

Okay, now finalize:

Final Summary:  
There is no evidence linking hepatitis G virus or TT virus to cryptogenic chronic liver disease, as studies show HPgV-1 does not establish hepatopathy independently and TT virus remains unassociated with such conditions. \boxed{B}

Thus:

Final Answer:  
Heights G virus has not been established as a cause of hepatitis or liver disease independently, and there is no documentation connecting either Hepatitis G virus or TT virus to cryptogenic chronic liver disease. \boxed{B}
